queries.go:16-34: SearchIssues calls searchWisps and if it returns an error, silently falls through to query the issues table instead. The error is completely discarded. This was added for backwards compatibility with pre-migration databases, but it masks real errors (query timeouts, connection failures, corrupt data). A timeout on the wisps query would result in silently returning stale/wrong results from the issues table. Suggested fix: distinguish between 'table does not exist' errors (legitimate fallthrough) and real errors (should be returned). Same pattern at lines 29-34 for allEphemeral(filter.IDs).","design":"","due_at":null,"ephemeral":0,"estimated_minutes":null,"event_kind":"","external_ref":null,"hook_bead":"","id":"bd-w59l","is_template":0,"issue_type":"task","last_activity":null,"metadata":"{}","mol_type":"","notes":"","original_size":null,"owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","payload":"","pinned":0,"priority":2,"quality_score":null,"rig":"","role_bead":"","role_type":"","sender":"","source_repo":"","source_system":"","spec_id":"","status":"closed","target":"","timeout_ns":0,"title":"SearchIssues ephemeral fallthrough silently swallows errors","updated_at":"2026-03-01T21:31:11Z","waiters":"","wisp_type":"","work_type":""}

+{"acceptance_criteria":"","actor":"","agent_state":"","assignee":"beads/refinery","await_id":"","await_type":"","close_reason":"","closed_at":null,"closed_by_session":"","compacted_at":null,"compacted_at_commit":null,"compaction_level":0,"content_hash":"869d16695065d79827ad7c114784e35b1e6677fcd18c142261646c02efbe4b95","created_at":"2026-03-05T23:53:17Z","created_by":"","crystallizes":0,"defer_until":null,"description":"attached_molecule: mol-refinery-patrol
attached_at: 2026-03-05T23:55:06Z

Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).","design":"","due_at":null,"ephemeral":0,"estimated_minutes":null,"event_kind":"","external_ref":null,"hook_bead":"","id":"bd-wisp-3cjw","is_template":0,"issue_type":"epic","last_activity":null,"metadata":"{}","mol_type":"","notes":"","original_size":null,"owner":"","payload":"","pinned":0,"priority":2,"quality_score":null,"rig":"","role_bead":"","role_type":"","sender":"","source_repo":"","source_system":"","spec_id":"","status":"pinned","target":"","timeout_ns":0,"title":"mol-refinery-patrol","updated_at":"2026-03-05T23:55:07Z","waiters":"","wisp_type":"","work_type":""}
 {"acceptance_criteria":"","actor":"","agent_state":"","assignee":"beads/polecats/opal","await_id":"","await_type":"","close_reason":"Closed","closed_at":"2026-03-01T18:55:46Z","closed_by_session":"","compacted_at":null,"compacted_at_commit":null,"compaction_level":0,"content_hash":"f10b368d3837269ed08ff419f440a042c5179d769539bfeb372eaf28a68053e2","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:07:33Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","crystallizes":0,"defer_until":null,"description":"In credentials.go withPeerCredentials (line 283), when a peer has no credentials (peer.Username and peer.Password are both empty), the code skips the mutex and runs fn() without any locking. But if another goroutine concurrently runs withPeerCredentials WITH credentials, the env vars could be set while this no-credential fn() is executing. The no-credential path's dolt CLI operations (if any) would inherit stale or concurrent credential env vars. The mutex should protect all federation operations, not just ones with explicit credentials.","design":"","due_at":null,"ephemeral":0,"estimated_minutes":null,"event_kind":"","external_ref":null,"hook_bead":"","id":"bd-wn3n","is_template":0,"issue_type":"task","last_activity":null,"metadata":"{}","mol_type":"","notes":"","original_size":null,"owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","payload":"","pinned":0,"priority":2,"quality_score":null,"rig":"","role_bead":"","role_type":"","sender":"","source_repo":"","source_system":"","spec_id":"","status":"closed","target":"","timeout_ns":0,"title":"withPeerCredentials: continues without mutex when peer has no credentials","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:55:46Z","waiters":"","wisp_type":"","work_type":""}
 {"acceptance_criteria":"","actor":"","agent_state":"","assignee":null,"await_id":"","await_type":"","close_reason":"","closed_at":"2026-03-01T18:21:41Z","closed_by_session":"","compacted_at":null,"compacted_at_commit":null,"compaction_level":0,"content_hash":"f4a5f7bc57f526898af87b967cbcbb97ebb98931bf93237a72d8aac5668b80ed","created_at":"2026-03-01T18:06:53Z","created_by":"beads/polecats/quartz","crystallizes":0,"defer_until":null,"description":"In credentials.go encryptionKey() (line 47), the AES-256 key is derived from SHA-256(dbPath + 'beads-federation-key-v1'). This means: (1) anyone who knows the dbPath can derive the key and decrypt stored passwords — the path is not a secret, (2) if the database directory is moved/renamed, all stored federation credentials become unreadable, (3) the static salt 'beads-federation-key-v1' provides no real entropy. The comment acknowledges this ('For production, consider using system keyring or external secret managers') but this should be tracked. Stored credentials in the federation_peers table are effectively obfuscated, not encrypted.","design":"","due_at":null,"ephemeral":0,"estimated_minutes":null,"event_kind":"","external_ref":null,"hook_bead":"","id":"bd-wvxf","is_template":0,"issue_type":"task","last_activity":null,"metadata":"{}","mol_type":"","notes":"","original_size":null,"owner":"steve.yegge@gmail.com","payload":"","pinned":0,"priority":2,"quality_score":null,"rig":"","role_bead":"","role_type":"","sender":"","source_repo":"","source_system":"","spec_id":"","status":"closed","target":"","timeout_ns":0,"title":"credentials.go: encryption key derived from dbPath is weak and portable","updated_at":"2026-03-01T18:21:41Z","waiters":"","wisp_type":"","work_type":""}
